entries.claim: Default product builds ignore a synthetic local Claude credential file.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- test -p isyncyou-app-host --features agent-oauth-providers product_build_does_not_read_dot_claude -- --nocapture

entries.claim: Default product builds ignore a synthetic local Codex credential file.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- test -p isyncyou-app-host --features agent-oauth-providers product_build_does_not_read_dot_codex -- --nocapture

entries.claim: An encrypted product credential takes precedence over the experimental desktop fallback.
A corrupt present product credential fails closed instead of falling through to local CLI auth.
Experimental local CLI credentials cannot satisfy product harness readiness.
The app-host production source has no subscription credential ingest method or route.
The mobile crate exposes no local-client experimental feature or generic fallback listener.
Android's bounded Cargo feature allowlist rejects the desktop experimental feature.
The drift reducer does not copy authorization material or account identifiers.
Unknown future usage fields force manual drift review without copying their names or values.
Claude's provider-required billing block remains first and exact.
Codex subscription/account identity headers remain in the expected provider envelope.
Provider requests contain no default-client prompt, tool, skill, MCP, rule, or history component.
Claude and Codex custom requests install only the controlled iSyncYou prompt and one isyncyou tool.
The product host rejects OAuth endpoint, client, scope, and redirect overrides.
Claude Code 2.1.207 produced a controlled, allowlist-reduced local drift summary with no retained raw capture.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- build -p isyncyou-agent --example reduce_local_drift --features agent-subscription-experimental && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh capture /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && jq -S . /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review/*.json && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh publish /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-627 APPROVE_REDUCED_SUMMARIES && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review
entries.notes: This is local-client drift evidence only, not product authentication evidence. Headers and endpoint were not safely visible.

entries.claim: Codex CLI 0.144.1 produced a controlled, allowlist-reduced local drift summary with no retained raw capture.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- build -p isyncyou-agent --example reduce_local_drift --features agent-subscription-experimental && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh capture /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review && jq -S . /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review/*.json && tools/capture-agent-local-drift.sh publish /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review docs/evidence/artifacts/issue-627 APPROVE_REDUCED_SUMMARIES && rm -rf /tmp/isyncyou-627-evidence-review
entries.evidence: controlled_sentinel_observed=true; raw_retained=false; drift_decision=not_safely_observable; model catalog reduced to counts/presence
entries.notes: This is local-client drift evidence only, not product authentication evidence. Headers and endpoint were not safely visible.

entries.claim: Cargo feature resolution excludes the fallback by default, enables it through one explicit daemon chain, and rejects it on mobile.
entries.command: cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1; cargo tree -p isyncyou-daemon -e normal -f '{p} features={f}'; cargo tree -p isyncyou-daemon --features agent-subscription-experimental -e normal -f '{p} features={f}'; cargo remote -c -- check -p isyncyou-mobile --features agent-subscription-experimental
entries.evidence: Default graph has no experimental feature; explicit graph resolves daemon to app-host to Agent; mobile rejects the absent feature with exit 101 before artifact creation.

entries.claim: Default desktop and Android product artifacts exclude local fallback and import markers.
entries.command: cargo remote -c -- build --release -p isyncyou-daemon; strings target/release/isyncyoud <forbidden-marker-scan>; (cd android && env -u ISY_CARGO_FEATURES ./gradlew clean :app:assembleDebug); unzip packaged libisyncyou_mobile.so; strings <forbidden-marker-scan>
entries.evidence: Default daemon and clean default APK built successfully; both native scans returned zero forbidden markers; hashes are recorded in the artifact.

entries.claim: Product copy/workflows and reduced public evidence expose neither the experimental setup nor credential/account/raw-capture values.
entries.command: python3 -m unittest tools.test_agent_experimental_boundary -v; scoped product-source/workflow/summarized-scalar scans
entries.evidence: Product source matches=0; product workflow matches=0; forbidden reduced-summary scalar matches=0; raw capture artifacts=0.

entries.claim: A partial Claude capture containing only authorization cannot be classified as no drift.
Removing any one required Claude wire header leaves the capture not safely observable.
Removing any one required Codex wire header leaves the capture not safely observable.
The drift reducer rejects a direct input symlink even when both link and target are below /tmp.
A corrupt product credential emits error followed by done(error), never a successful FakeProvider turn.
An expired non-refreshable product credential emits error followed by done(error), never a successful FakeProvider turn.
Persisted Codex callback diagnostics contain only a fixed safe-boolean schema, with no callback URL, code, state, or error text.
The product-source scanner retains production code after early cfg(test) items and excludes only the final test module.
The drift reducer's exact required header sets remain synchronized with the Claude and Codex runtime builders.
App-host startup removes any legacy Codex callback diagnostics file that may contain an old callback target.
